data.title: SharonAI Clarifies Misstatement on NVIDIA Shareholder Relationship
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from SharonAI Holdings ( (SHAZ) ).

SharonAI Holdings, Inc. disclosed that its Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, filed on March 31, 2026, mistakenly described NVIDIA as a strategic shareholder. The company clarified that NVIDIA was never a strategic shareholder and currently holds no equity securities in SharonAI, emphasizing that the correction does not affect any previously reported financial statements or other disclosures in the 10-K.

The revision underscores that SharonAI’s earlier reference to NVIDIA’s shareholder status was an isolated disclosure error rather than an accounting or operational issue. Investors and other stakeholders are being assured that the company’s financial reporting remains intact, with no restatements or broader changes required beyond correcting the misstatement about NVIDIA’s relationship with the firm.

The most recent analyst rating on (SHAZ) stock is a Buy with a $40.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SharonAI Holdings stock, see the SHAZ Stock Forecast page.

More about SharonAI Holdings

SharonAI Holdings, Inc. is a company that files periodic reports as a public issuer, including an Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company’s latest filing activity indicates it is subject to U.S. securities reporting requirements and engages with strategic technology sector stakeholders, though specific products, services, or markets were not detailed in this release.

data.title: SharonAI Plans Dual Listing and Australian Capital Raising
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

The latest announcement is out from SharonAI Holdings ( (SHAZ) ).

On April 13, 2026, SharonAI Holdings Inc. announced plans to seek a dual listing of its securities on the Australian Securities Exchange in the form of CHESS Depositary Interests, each representing one Class A Ordinary Common share, alongside its existing Nasdaq listing. In conjunction with the proposed ASX admission, the company plans an Australian public offering of CDIs and a concurrent private offering to institutional investors in certain other countries, with existing convertible promissory notes expected to automatically convert into CDIs immediately before ASX listing, a move that could broaden its investor base, deepen access to Australian capital markets and streamline its capital structure, although completion, pricing and proceeds of the offering remain uncertain.

More about SharonAI Holdings

SharonAI Holdings Inc., listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker SHAZ, is a high‑performance computing company focused on artificial intelligence and cloud GPU compute infrastructure. An announcement from Banco Santander Chile ( (BSAC) ) is now available.

Banco Santander Chile reported consolidated financial information as of March 31, 2026, showing total assets of CLP 69.9 trillion, driven mainly by CLP 39.5 trillion in customer and bank loans and CLP 9.6 trillion in financial instruments. The bank’s balance sheet also reflects sizable derivative positions and a solid equity base of CLP 4.9 trillion, underscoring its scale in the local market.

For the period ended March 2026, the bank generated net income of CLP 280.5 billion, supported by CLP 489.5 billion in net interest income and CLP 154.9 billion in fee and commission income. After provisions for loan losses and operating expenses, income before tax reached CLP 336.4 billion, with most earnings attributable to the bank’s shareholders, indicating resilient profitability and stable operations in the first quarter of 2026.

data.title: Kazia Therapeutics Licenses SETDB1 Epigenetic Platform to Bolster Oncology Pipeline
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Kazia Therapeutics ( (KZIA) ).

On April 10, 2026, Kazia Therapeutics entered a license and commercialization agreement with QIMR Berghofer Medical Research Institute to acquire exclusive global rights to a first-in-class SETDB1-targeted epigenetic drug development platform. The deal includes an upfront payment of about $1.39 million and a tiered revenue-sharing structure, adding lead candidate MSETC and an AI-integrated discovery engine to Kazia’s portfolio.

The SETDB1 platform targets a key epigenetic regulator of tumor immune evasion, aiming to restore immune signaling and overcome resistance to immunotherapies, including checkpoint inhibitors. Kazia plans to advance the SETDB1 program, now in preclinical development, in parallel with its nuclear PD-L1 degrader, expecting to bring both to IND readiness in roughly 18 months at a combined cost of about $6 million, strengthening its differentiated, multi-layered oncology platform and enhancing partnering potential in a $15–20 billion epigenetic therapeutics market.

The most recent analyst rating on (KZIA) stock is a Buy with a $18.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Kazia Therapeutics stock, see the KZIA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on KZIA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, KZIA is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (minimal revenue, large ongoing losses, and persistent cash burn with negative equity). Technicals are a notable offset with strong momentum above major moving averages, but overbought signals add near-term risk; valuation remains challenged due to negative earnings and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on KZIA stock, click here.

More about Kazia Therapeutics

Kazia Therapeutics Limited is a Sydney-based, clinical-stage oncology-focused drug development company listed on Nasdaq. Its lead asset is paxalisib, a brain-penetrant PI3K/Akt/mTOR inhibitor in multiple cancer trials, alongside pipeline assets EVT801 and NDL2 targeting VEGFR3 and nuclear PD-L1 to address treatment resistance in aggressive tumors.

The company operates in the oncology therapeutics market, targeting solid and brain tumors with a focus on treatment-refractory disease and immunotherapy resistance. With regulatory designations including Orphan Drug and Fast Track for paxalisib in several indications, Kazia is positioning itself within a large and growing global market for next-generation, mechanism-driven cancer therapies.

Average Trading Volume: 190,977

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $99.98M

data.title: Catalyst Crew Expands Venezuelan AI Healthcare Operations Structure
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Catalyst Crew Technologies ( (CCTC) ) has issued an update.

On February 17, 2026, Catalyst Crew Technologies entered into an Asset Purchase Agreement with CEO Kevin Rodan Levy to acquire intellectual property and other assets related to its AI-enabled healthcare analytics platform, with the transaction closing around February 20, 2026. The assets included proprietary software, machine-learning models, datasets, and Venezuelan patent registrations associated with CardioAI, PulmoAI, and NeuroAI.

On March 23, 2026, the company acquired 100% of the shares of Venezuelan corporation Inversiones Long 33, C.A., making it a wholly owned subsidiary and designated operating entity in Venezuela within its Latin American strategy. On April 7, 2026, the previously acquired IP for CardioAI, PulmoAI, and NeuroAI was internally reassigned to this subsidiary without additional consideration as an organizational realignment step.

Between March 23 and April 13, 2026, the company issued a series of press releases outlining the establishment of its Venezuelan operating structure, the IP assignment, and the market introduction of its CardioAI, PulmoAI, and NeuroAI platforms. These moves collectively formalize Catalyst Crew Technologies’ ownership and deployment structure for its core AI healthcare technologies in Venezuela, potentially strengthening its regional positioning and operational clarity for stakeholders.

More about Catalyst Crew Technologies

Catalyst Crew Technologies Corp. operates in the artificial intelligence-enabled healthcare technology industry, focusing on analytics platforms for cardiovascular, pulmonary, and neurological applications. Its primary products include the CardioAI, PulmoAI, and NeuroAI platforms, and the company is building an operating presence in Venezuela as part of a broader Latin American growth strategy.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell
